code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test13-SEP-2023Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, ketonuria, glucosuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), indicating possible nephrotic syndrome or acute kidney injury. Low HbA1c with glucosuria may reflect recent glycemic changes or renal glycosuria. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for underlying metabolic or renal pathology.
